title: Telegram quote-reply context not injected into Pi dispatch â€” Pi ignores what Ash is replying to
source: Ash live report 2026-06-22 â€” when Ash quote-replies to Pi's message, Pi responds as if it has no idea which message is being referenced.
current_state: BubbleMessage.reply_to_text captures the quoted text from message.reply_to_message.text. bubble.reply_targets exposes these as a list. _dispatch_bubble's _do_recall thread searches L3/L2 memory semantically for these texts. However: (1) L3/L2 only contains distilled durable facts, NOT recent conversation turns â€” a Pi reply from 30 seconds ago has not been distilled yet, so recall_referenced finds nothing. (2) Even when recall fires, the quoted text itself is never injected into the dispatched message â€” it goes through a semantic lookup that usually returns empty. (3) bubble.joined_text() only joins m.text, dropping m.reply_to_text entirely. Result: Pi has no idea which of its own messages Ash is replying to.
target_state: When a bubble message has reply_to_text set, prepend a '[Replying to: "..."]' context line directly into the joined text that is dispatched to Pi. No semantic lookup needed â€” we already have the exact text. This gives Pi unambiguous context about which message is being quoted. The existing _do_recall path can remain as a supplemental L3 lookup but is not the primary mechanism.
migration_plan: 1. In _dispatch_bubble, replace the single line `joined = bubble.joined_text()` with a loop that builds joined text including reply context: for each BubbleMessage, if reply_to_text is set prepend '[Replying to: "<reply_to_text[:300]>"]' before the message text.
2. Keep the existing _do_recall semantic search as a best-effort supplement (it may find relevant L3 facts when the quoted text matches a durable memory). Do not remove it.
3. Also handle the case where reply_to_text comes from a Pi message that was a button or action result â€” strip leading formatting markers before injecting.

4. Tests: test_telegram_quote_reply.py â€” (a) a BubbleMessage with reply_to_text produces a joined string that includes the '[Replying to: ...]' prefix; (b) a BubbleMessage without reply_to_text produces unchanged joined text; (c) multiple messages in a bubble, one with reply, one without â€” only the one with reply gets the prefix.
risk_notes: Keep the injected quote short (300 chars) to avoid prompt bloat. The '[Replying to: ...]' marker is already used in the callback handler ('[Button selected: ...]') so Pi already understands bracketed context markers â€” no prompt change needed.
